---
title: "zew1602a using ndiswrapper on 64bit ubuntu ??"
date: 2008-03-12
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by hellmet on 2008-03-12
Well, my Zonet zew1602a supplied the 64bit drivers for Windows. I tried installing them using ndiswrapper GUI. I can't see my card in Networking
 Using 32 bit drivers gave me an error : 
 ndiswrapper (check_nt_hdr:153): kernel is 64-bit, but Windows driver is not 64-bit;bad magic: 010B
[  355.623460] ndiswrapper (load_sys_files:216): couldn't prepare driver 'netmw125'
[  355.624195] ndiswrapper (load_wrap_driver:118): couldn't load driver netmw125; check system log for messages from 'loadndisdriver'
[  355.624869] usbcore: registered new interface driver ndiswrapper

Are there additional steps to be performed like loading the modules etc?
Please let me know.

---

### Post by hellmet on 2008-03-12
Okay. I rebooted and tried 
>  modprobe ndiswrapperand I got this :
> [  128.589174] ndiswrapper version 1.45 loaded (smp=yes)
[  128.637976] ndiswrapper (link_pe_images:577): fixing KI_USER_SHARED_DATA address in the driver
[  128.639032] ndiswrapper: driver netmw126 (Marvell,12/30/2005,3.2.3.2) loaded
[  128.640905] ndiswrapper: using IRQ 19
[  128.904860] usbcore: registered new interface driver ndiswrapper

I am now able to see wireless networks when I go to Networking> Wireless > SSID. When I put in the password and my network, it seems like its connecting. But, I get no internet. 
Now dmesg is a mess :
> [  128.640905] ndiswrapper: using IRQ 19
[  128.903560] wlan0: ethernet device 08:10:74:0e:fd:5c using NDIS driver: netmw126, version: 0x3020007, NDIS v&#9496;4sion: 0x501, vendor: 'q7p7&#9147;7&#9147;6&#9146;6&#9532;6&#9492;6&#9484;6
